QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_29

question.text: A stock's Market Wide Position Limit (MWPL) is 1,00,00,000 shares (1 crore). The current aggregate open interest across all exchanges is 96,00,000 shares. What trading restriction applies?

answer.text: The stock enters a ban period since aggregate OI (96%) exceeds 95% of MWPL; only offsetting (square-off) trades are permitted
No restriction — the ban triggers only when OI reaches 100% of MWPL
New positions are restricted only for the exchange where OI is highest, not across all exchanges
The stock enters risk reduction mode where margins are increased by 50%, but new positions are still allowed

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When aggregate open interest across all exchanges reaches or exceeds 95% of MWPL, the stock enters a ban period. At 96,00,000 / 1,00,00,000 = 96%, which exceeds 95%, the ban is triggered. Only offsetting/square-off positions are allowed; no new positions. **The Trap:** The ban threshold is 95% of MWPL, not 100%. Also, the ban is market-wide (across ALL exchanges), not exchange-specific. Risk reduction mode (at 90%) is a different concept specific to clearing member utilization. **Cross-Exam Context:** MWPL ban (95%) is equity-specific. Currency derivatives use absolute USD position limits without a ban mechanism. The 90%/85% risk reduction mode applies to clearing member collateral utilization. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Penalty for taking new positions during ban period is 1% of order value per day. The ban is lifted only when aggregate OI falls below 80% of MWPL — note the asymmetric thresholds (95% entry, 80% exit).
